name: Andex AI — GitHub for Engineering Decisions
vision: Version-control engineering decisions and institutional knowledge so software teams keep a shared mental model as projects evolve. Every change is proposed, analysed by specialist AI agents, voted on by the team, decided by a manager, tracked on a decision branch, and archived — with drift between the agreed model and reality detected automatically.
goals: Eliminate mental model drift by making the agreed project state visible and versioned
Keep humans accountable for every decision — AI recommends, humans approve
Make every AI action and permission decision auditable
Turn user feedback into evidence-backed, governable feature proposals
Protect institutional knowledge with deterministic, role-based memory governance
functionalRequirements: FR-001: Managers can create a project by uploading a project brief document
FR-002: Uploaded briefs are parsed into name, vision, goals, functional and non-functional requirements
FR-003: All team members can view each project's vision, goals, requirements, architecture, memory, and version
FR-004: Managers can update a project's editable fields
FR-005: Functional and non-functional requirements are presented as a distinct per-project view
FR-006: Team members can submit suggestions targeting a project's main idea or an open decision branch
FR-007: Each new suggestion is automatically enriched with related decisions, architecture, and a context summary
FR-008: Likely duplicate suggestions are flagged against existing active suggestions
FR-009: Each suggestion receives an impact analysis covering dependencies, architecture, APIs, tasks, cost, and risk
FR-010: Each suggestion receives an AI review with pros, cons, risks, trade-offs, questions, and a team summary
FR-011: Workers can cast one revisable vote per suggestion (approve, reject, needs discussion, approve with comments); managers cannot vote
FR-012: Vote tallying routes suggestions to 'ready for manager', or 'needs discussion' when rejections outnumber approvals — never auto-approval
FR-013: Only a manager can accept a tallied suggestion, recording the decision and an optional note
FR-014: Only a manager can decline a tallied suggestion with a note, leaving projects and branches unchanged
FR-015: Accepting a main-targeting suggestion creates a decision branch with the merged content, leaving main unchanged
FR-016: Accepting a branch-targeting suggestion merges it into that branch and increments the branch version
FR-017: Managers can start branch implementation, generating linked implementation tasks
FR-018: Managers can merge an open or implementing branch to main, updating the project content and version
FR-019: Managers can discard an open or implementing branch without any change to main
FR-020: All decision branches are viewable with status, versions, and accepted suggestions
FR-021: Implementation tasks are viewable with status, branch linkage, and affected components
FR-022: Drift scans raise alerts for tasks referencing unknown components and for excessive open-suggestion backlogs
FR-023: Every AI agent action is logged immutably and the log is visible to the whole team
FR-024: A catalogue of each specialist agent's declared skills is published
FR-025: Key workflow moments record notification intents (channel and message) without asserting delivery
FR-026: User feedback items are ingested with source, sentiment, segment, and geography for product discovery
FR-027: Related feedback is clustered into feature packs with evidence, segments, pros/cons/risks, and priority, confidence, and impact ratings
FR-028: Feature packs can be promoted into suggestions or dismissed by a human — never automatically
FR-029: Knowledge resources are registered as governed memory with visibility, allowed roles, owner, optional unlock time, and lineage
FR-030: Access to governed memory is decided deterministically before any content reaches an AI model
FR-031: Derived memory inherits the most restrictive permissions along its lineage
FR-032: Non-public governed memory under a temporal lock is inaccessible before its unlock time
FR-033: Every permission decision — grant or deny — is audit-logged with user, resource, action, outcome, and reason
FR-034: Team members can ask project questions answered only from their authorised memory, with provider and filter counts reported
FR-035: A governance dashboard shows governed records, effective permissions, and the permission audit log
FR-036: AI completions use interchangeable providers with automatic fallback and graceful degradation when none is available
FR-037: The active team member identity can be switched among predefined roles, immediately changing permissions (demo scope)
FR-038: A predefined test-organisation dataset can be loaded that exercises the full workflow including edge cases
nonFunctionalRequirements: NFR-001 Performance: suggestion analysis completes at p95 ≤ 5s with AI, ≤ 1s without
NFR-002 Performance: governed question answering responds at p95 ≤ 8s including provider fallback
NFR-003 Reliability: a single AI provider outage causes zero failed user actions when a fallback provider is configured
NFR-004 Reliability: invalid workflow transitions are always rejected; the edge-case suite passes 100%
NFR-005 Security: provider credentials never appear in any API response, page, or version-controlled file
NFR-006 Privacy: zero unauthorised memory records ever reach an AI model, across all roles
NFR-007 Auditability: 100% of agent actions and permission decisions produce reconstructable log entries
NFR-008 Availability: the app remains usable with no AI provider and an empty database, with empty-state messaging
NFR-009 Robustness: empty, oversized (20k chars), unicode, and injection-shaped input never corrupts state
NFR-010 Versioning: every change to agreed project content traces to a branch merge with a version increment
NFR-011 Maintainability: adding an OpenAI-compatible provider requires only a registry entry and environment configuration
NFR-012 AI Explainability: every AI output discloses its producing agent or provider; governed answers report used/filtered memory counts
NFR-013 Observability: provider and database status are queryable at runtime without exposing secrets
NFR-014 Compliance: governed memory metadata can express residency and classification constraints on 100% of records
